description: context-project returns the project name for a path inside a registered project
steps: Set up a temp directory registered as a known project (via PROJECT_<KEY>_* env vars)
Run: context-project <path-inside-project>
Assert output equals the project name

description: context-project returns empty string for a path outside all registered projects
steps: Run: context-project /tmp/unregistered-dir
Assert output is empty

description: context-root returns the Worktree root when given a path inside a linked Worktree
steps: Create a bare repo with a linked worktree checked out at a known path
Run: context-root <path-inside-worktree>
Assert output equals the worktree root directory path

description: context-root returns the Project root when given a path inside a project but not a Worktree
steps: Register a plain (non-worktree) git repo as a project
Run: context-root <path-inside-project>
Assert output equals the project root directory path

description: context-root returns empty string for a path outside all known projects
steps: Run: context-root /tmp/unregistered-dir
Assert output is empty

description: context-path returns the sub-path relative to the Context Root
steps: Register a project with root at /some/project
Run: context-path /some/project/src/foo
Assert output equals src/foo (no leading slash)

description: context-badge output contains project name and no branch for a path inside a Git Repo Main
steps: Register a plain git repo as a project
Run: context-badge <path-inside-project>
Assert output contains the project name
Assert output does not contain any branch name

description: context-badge output contains both project name and branch name for a path inside a Worktree
steps: Create a linked worktree on a known branch for a registered project
Run: context-badge <path-inside-worktree>
Assert output contains the project name
Assert output contains the worktree branch name

description: context-badge accepts a project name and produces the same output as the project root path
steps: Run: context-badge <project-name>
Run: context-badge <project-root-path>
Assert both outputs are identical

description: context-badge returns empty output for a path outside all known projects
steps: Run: context-badge /tmp/unregistered-dir
Assert output is empty

description: context-badge output contains raw ANSI escape sequences and not zsh %K{ codes
steps: Run: context-badge <any-registered-path>
Assert output contains ESC[ ANSI sequences
Assert output does not contain %K{

description: context-badge --zsh output contains %K{ zsh prompt color codes
steps: Run: context-badge <any-registered-path> --zsh
Assert output contains %K{

description: context-badge without --zsh output does not contain %K{ codes
steps: Run: context-badge <any-registered-path>
Assert output does not contain %K{

description: prompt path part shows project name only when inside a Git Repo Main
steps: Enter a registered plain git repo (non-worktree)
Assert prompt path part contains the project name
Assert prompt path part does not contain a branch name

description: prompt path part shows project name and branch when inside a linked Worktree
steps: Enter a linked worktree on a known branch
Assert prompt path part contains the project name
Assert prompt path part contains the branch name

description: sub-path shown after the badge is relative to the Context Root
steps: Enter a subdirectory inside a linked worktree
Assert the sub-path displayed is relative to the worktree root, not the project root

description: no async git_worktree_branch prompt part remains
steps: Assert git_worktree_branch is not referenced in the prompt configuration
Assert git_worktree_branch tests are removed from git.zsh.bats

description: FZF file picker prompt shows Context Badge and sub-path relative to Context Root
steps: Open FZF file picker inside a registered project directory
Assert prompt contains the Context Badge
Assert sub-path shown is relative to the Context Root

description: FZF preview header shows Context Badge and file path relative to Context Root
steps: Open FZF file picker and preview a file inside a registered project
Assert preview header contains the Context Badge
Assert file path shown is relative to the Context Root

description: FZF Claude session list shows Context Badge per session working directory
steps: Open FZF Claude sessions list
Assert each entry shows the Context Badge for its working directory

description: FZF projects list shows Project Badge per project with no Worktree Badge
steps: Open FZF projects list
Assert each entry shows the project badge (no branch suffix)

description: no remaining calls to project-by-path in FZF functions
steps: Assert no FZF function references project-by-path

description: NeoVim statusline calls context-project instead of project-by-path
steps: Assert statusline.lua references context-project
Assert statusline.lua does not reference project-by-path

description: project-by-path autoload function is deleted and has no remaining references
steps: Assert project-by-path autoload function file does not exist
Assert no file in the codebase references project-by-path

description: statusline shows Context Badge as a single unit (project + branch when in worktree)
steps: Open Claude Code inside a linked worktree
Assert statusline shows both project name and branch name as one badge

description: statusline shows project name only when inside a Git Repo Main
steps: Open Claude Code inside a plain git repo (non-worktree)
Assert statusline shows project name without branch

description: no separate branch call remains in the statusline
steps: Assert statusline configuration does not contain a separate git-branch-current or git-branch-colorize call
